paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VIII. Excluding a path and a complete multipartite graph
summary: Conjecture 1.6 from arXiv:2303.11766 — that every forest is multibounding — remains open as of May 2026. The source paper itself established the conjecture for paths and brooms, and earlier work (arXiv:2202.05557) handled trees of radius two; no subsequent paper resolving the full conjecture has been identified in a broad web search covering arXiv, Seymour's publication list, and related follow-up work through 2025.
search_queries: multibounding forest chromatic number Nguyen Scott Seymour 2303.11766
"multibounding" graph forest H-free chromatic number polynomial bound arxiv
"every forest is multibounding" OR "multibounding" forest Nguyen Scott Seymour 2024 2025
"polynomial bounds chromatic number" "IX" OR "9" forest multibounding Nguyen Scott Seymour arxiv 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper proving or disproving the full conjecture was found. Known special cases: paths (proved in the source paper), brooms (proved in the source paper), trees of radius two (proved in arXiv:2202.05557 by Scott and Seymour). The conjecture is closely related to the polynomial Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture; any forest satisfying the polynomial-bound Conjecture 1.2 of the same paper is automatically multibounding.
